description: Individual GenAI assistant for US laws and regulations; evaluation tier for Reggi UX
how_it_works: Summarize/simplify text; query US laws; verifiable citations on responses; no Smart Law Library or change-management features

description: US-only change management with full Smart Law Library; Reggi scoped to library and alerts
how_it_works: Smart Law Library US; regulatory change management and impact assessments; Reggi scoped to library + alerts; US Federal + 50 states; dashboards; in-app support
list_price_or_pricing_note: $1700 per user per month (3-year contract); Tooliverse 2026 confirms; SmartSuite third-party cited ~$1250 as alternate
notes_for_team: High entry price; GLI build-vs-buy argument in report §3.4

description: Global program: multi-jurisdiction; bills; GRC; compliance management; custom API; 24/7 support
how_it_works: Everything in Professional plus global (135+ countries); bills and agency updates; customizable topics; 16M+ doc search; group workflows; risk/task/policy/controls; GRC connectors; REST/JSON XML CSV SFTP; customized legal research support
architecture_mapping: Enterprise integration + global corpus + compliance objects
list_price_or_pricing_note: Custom (contact sales); no published list price
notes_for_team: Features GLI needs most (global; GLIAccess-style integrations) sit in highest-cost tier per §3.4

description: Third-party review themes affecting total cost of ownership
how_it_works: Sticker shock at Professional; non-GRC sync less reliable than native GRC; HITL still required; niche/regional coverage vs curated legal DBs
notes_for_team: Share with procurement and architecture risk reviews

description: High-level flow from sources through agents to GRC and API
how_it_works: Primary sources → ingestion + NLP/ML normalization → per-customer Smart Law Library → three agents (Change; Compliance; Research/Reggi) → alerts/drafts/Q&A → HITL → GRC connectors + dashboards → Open API
architecture_mapping: Sources → ingest → SLL → RCA/CA/RRA → HITL → GRC → API (mermaid §4.1)
notes_for_team: Regology does not publish detailed internal diagram; this is reconstructed from public docs + reviews

description: NLP/ML reads law like a lawyer (structure; definitions; shall/must; penalties; exemptions; cross-refs); GenAI (Reggi) wraps layer for summarization; drafting; Q&A with RAG citations
how_it_works: Clause/section parsing; multi-page regulatory updates synthesized vs prior version; three specialist agents coordinate retrieval + LLM + workflow
architecture_mapping: Ingest + NLP/ML core + Generative layer (Reggi) + agentic orchestration
notes_for_team: Key lesson: structural understanding not only embedding similarity

description: Proprietary per-customer filtered corpus over global content; paragraph-level citations; continuous updates vs full-doc replacement

notes_for_team: GLI analog: filtered gaming corpus + GLI standards/product taxonomy

description: Outbound formats and GRC connectors for systems of record
